justification: Official Senate press release from Booker's office explicitly names Booker, Heinrich, Lieu, and Obernolte as co-sponsors of the reintroduced AI Grand Challenges Act.
Same press release names Booker and Ted Lieu as co-sponsors of the reintroduced AI Grand Challenges Act.
Same press release names Booker and Jay Obernolte as co-sponsors of the reintroduced AI Grand Challenges Act.
Booker's official Senate press release explicitly names Booker, Heinrich, Todd Young, and Rounds as co-sponsors introducing the CREATE AI Act of 2023.
Eshoo's House press release names Eshoo, McCaul, Beyer, and Obernolte as introducing the House companion CREATE AI Act bill, directly evidencing Booker's Senate bill's House companion sponsorship.
arXiv page for 'Taking AI Welfare Seriously' lists Kyle Fish and David Chalmers among the co-authors, directly evidencing their collaboration on the paper.
arXiv page for 'Taking AI Welfare Seriously' lists Kyle Fish and Robert Long (Rob Long) as co-authors, directly evidencing their collaboration.

Eleos AI post describes Eleos (led by Rob Long) conducting model welfare assessments of Anthropic's Claude in collaboration with Kyle Fish's team, evidencing the Fish–Long collaboration on assessments.
Eleos AI post describes Eleos (co-founded by Kathleen Finlinson) conducting self-report model welfare assessments with Anthropic, evidencing the Fish–Finlinson collaboration.
Official Nobel Prize press release jointly names Daron Acemoglu, Simon Johnson, and James A. Robinson as co-recipients of the 2024 Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences.
MIT Shaping the Future of Work Initiative page names Daron Acemoglu, Simon Johnson, and David Autor as faculty co-directors, directly evidencing their co-director collaboration.
Axios article on Kratsios's OSTP confirmation explicitly states he will work closely with AI and crypto czar David Sacks on regulation and policy.
Wikipedia biography of Regina Barzilay explicitly states she spent a year as a postdoctoral researcher at Cornell University after her PhD.
Google DeepMind's official blog post announces that Demis Hassabis and John Jumper were jointly awarded the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold.
Wikipedia article on Timnit Gebru explicitly states she co-led Google's Ethical AI team with Margaret Mitchell before both were fired.
